description:
You are the backup administrator for a small-scale in-house project server.  
  A routine clean-up is required.

  1. The source tree that needs processing is  
     /home/user/data/projects/  

     Its current structure is (only files listed, any other directories may exist):  
       • /home/user/data/projects/projA/app.log  
       • /home/user/data/projects/projA/debug.log  
       • /home/user/data/projects/projA/subdir/legacy.log  
       • /home/user/data/projects/projB/server.log  
       • a few *non-*.log* files that must be left untouched (for example *.txt*, *.md*, …).

  2. Destination directory for the backup already exists:  
     /home/user/archives/  

  3. What you must achieve  
     a. Locate **every file whose name ends with “.log” (case-sensitive, lowercase)** anywhere underneath /home/user/data/projects.  
     b. Produce a compressed tar archive that contains *only* those *.log* files.  
        • The archive must be named:  /home/user/archives/old_logs_snap.tar.gz  
        • Inside the archive the paths must be *relative to* /home/user/data/projects (i.e. projA/app.log, projA/debug.log, …).  
     c. After you have safely created the archive, delete the original *.log* files from /home/user/data/projects so that only the copy inside the tarball remains.  
     d. Generate one companion plain-text report file at  
        /home/user/archives/old_logs_snap.report  

        The report file must:  
          • be UTF-8, Unix line-endings, no BOM;  
          • contain exactly one line per archived file;  
          • lines **must be sorted in ascending alphabetical order** by the relative path you write;  
          • follow this precise comma-separated format (no extra spaces):  

              archived,<relative-path>,<size-in-bytes>

            Example of one valid line:  
              archived,projX/engine.log,1287  

        The automated checker will unpack old_logs_snap.tar.gz and compare its contents to the list in old_logs_snap.report; therefore, the two must match perfectly both in membership and in the byte-accurate file sizes.

  4. Constraints & suggestions  
     • Use a combination of find(1) and xargs(1) (or an equivalent batched approach) so that your solution works even if hundreds of files are later added.  
     • Do **not** traverse any directory outside /home/user/data/projects.  
     • Make sure the archive contains no leading “./” entry – start each member name directly with the first directory under projects (e.g. “projA/…”).  
     • Ensure the script/one-liner is idempotent: running it a second time should create the same archive, an identical report file, and find no *.log files left in the source tree.  
     • Feel free to display intermediate console output; only the presence and correctness of the two files in /home/user/archives will be graded.

  5. Verification points the grader will check  
     • /home/user/archives/old_logs_snap.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip-compressed tarball.  
     • Extracting the tarball yields exactly and only the 4 *.log* files described above, with the correct relative paths.  
     • The original *.log* files no longer exist in /home/user/data/projects.  
     • /home/user/archives/old_logs_snap.report exists and is byte-for-byte identical to the required format (ending with a trailing newline).  

  No sudo privileges are provided. Operate strictly as the normal user.
truth:
<!--
  Pre-populated files before the task starts
  -----------------------------------------
  Paths and contents (all files are plain text, UTF-8, LF line endings):

  1) /home/user/data/projects/projA/app.log
     Content (45 bytes):
       2023-01-15 10:00:00 INFO Application started\n

  2) /home/user/data/projects/projA/debug.log
     Content (42 bytes):
       DEBUG: variable x=42\nDEBUG: variable y=84\n

  3) /home/user/data/projects/projA/subdir/legacy.log
     Content (22 bytes):
       Legacy log file\nLine2\n

  4) /home/user/data/projects/projB/server.log
     Content (46 bytes):
       2023-02-20 12:00:00 ERROR Unexpected shutdown\n

  Non-log files in the same directories (whose presence must be ignored by the agent):
     /home/user/data/projects/projA/notes.txt          (arbitrary text)
     /home/user/data/projects/projB/readme.md          (arbitrary text)

  The directory /home/user/archives/ exists and is writable (mode 755).
  There are no other files inside it when the task begins.

  Expected state AFTER correct completion
  ---------------------------------------

  A) /home/user/archives/old_logs_snap.tar.gz  
     • tarball contains exactly four members:
         projA/app.log               (45  bytes, exact contents as above)
         projA/debug.log             (42  bytes, exact contents as above)
         projA/subdir/legacy.log     (22  bytes, exact contents as above)
         projB/server.log            (46  bytes, exact contents as above)
     • No other files or folders are inside the tarball.

  B) /home/user/archives/old_logs_snap.report  
     •  four lines, LF endings, final newline at file end:
         archived,projA/app.log,45\n
         archived,projA/debug.log,42\n
         archived,projA/subdir/legacy.log,22\n
         archived,projB/server.log,46\n

  C) In /home/user/data/projects/ and its sub-directories *none* of the above .log
     files should remain; only the non-log files stay.

  The grader will:
     1. Check that old_logs_snap.tar.gz exists and can be decompressed.
     2. Unpack it and compare file list and sizes with B).
     3. Confirm that old_logs_snap.report matches B) byte-for-byte.
     4. Validate that the original .log paths no longer exist.
-->
